description: Transnational organized crime syndicate from Venezuela, founded in 2014 in Aragua state. Led by Héctor Rusthenford Guerrero Flores (alias Niño Guerrero) until his death in 2026. Operates from Tocorón prison, engaging in drug trafficking, human trafficking, murder, and other crimes. Expanded across Latin America and the United States during the Venezuelan refugee crisis. Designated as a terrorist organization by several countries.

country_links.evidence_quote: On 29 May 2025, Argentine Security Minister Patricia Bullrich announced the arrest of 12 alleged members of Tren de Aragua...
country_links.context: Argentine authorities arrested 12 alleged members of Tren de Aragua.
